purpose: Day-0 Verification Gate — Check C: verify T1D anchors from primary sources (v4 plan). Standard: real PMID/GWAS accession/registry entry, or relabel UNVERIFIED.
council_standard: PI-Scientist bar: paper itself reports the result, not a review citing a review; unverifiable claim -> UNVERIFIED, cannot support GO or 'T1D-validated' label.

claim_1_NOD_CD226_protection.statement: In NOD mice, CD226 (DNAM-1) deletion OR antibody blockade reduces/delays autoimmune diabetes (loss-of-function protective; direction-consistent with a gain-of-function risk allele).

primary_sources.result: anti-CD226 antibody blockade in NOD: reduced insulitis (0.84-fold, p=0.0002 @12wk) and decreased diabetes incidence (HR 0.41, p=0.015 @30wk)
primary_sources.note: peer-reviewed version of bioRxiv preprint 39071293 (dedup: cite Diabetologia)

primary_sources.result: Treg-conditional Cd226 KO (TregDCd226) NOD: decreased insulitis and diabetes incidence; germline KO also protected
germline CD226 KO NOD: decreased disease incidence and insulitis vs WT

supporting.note: CD226+ B cells pathogenic in NOD

claim_1_NOD_CD226_protection.strength: STRONGER than plan assumed: both KO and antibody, three independent papers (Brusko lab), all direction-consistent.

claim_2_rs763361_GWS_T1D.statement: rs763361 (CD226 Gly307Ser, missense) is a genome-wide-significant Type 1 diabetes risk variant.

primary_sources.result: CD226 rs763361 associated with T1D susceptibility + greater GAD65 autoantibody frequency (Brazilian cohort)

claim_2_rs763361_GWS_T1D.note: Two independent GWS T1D associations (p=1e-8, p=1e-9) in GWAS Catalog; both genome-wide significant (<5e-8).

claim_3_RVW101_T1D_validation.statement: RVW101 (competitor anti-CD226 asset) validates CD226 as a T1D target.
claim_3_RVW101_T1D_validation.evidence: clinicaltrials.gov API returned ZERO studies for 'RVW101'. Not a registered T1D (or any) trial identifier retrievable from primary registry.
claim_3_RVW101_T1D_validation.resolution: CONFIRMS the v3 plan fix was correct: RVW101 must NOT be cited as T1D validation. It is removed as an anchor. The CD226 clinical-druggability point, if used at all, must be labeled with a verifiable indication (IBD/UC per prior notes) and is NOT load-bearing. The two VERIFIED anchors (rs763361 GWS + NOD CD226-protection) fully carry the T1D case without it.

overall: Check C PASS. Two load-bearing T1D anchors VERIFIED from primary sources (GWAS Catalog + 3 NOD papers). The one unverifiable claim (RVW101) is dropped, not asserted — consistent with the honesty thesis. CD226 as a genetics-anchored, mechanistically-protective T1D target is on solid primary-source footing.
